content.text:
Module:
Cryptography and Computer Security  |
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF32
VHS:
84h  |
Weekly hours:
6h  |
Lectures:
3h  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Introduce students to the fundamental principles of computer security and cryptography
Master data protection techniques (encryption, authentication, firewalls, IDS/IPS, etc.)
Raise awareness of threats, attacks and best practices for securing systems and networks
Recommended Prerequisites
Notions of computer networks and computer systems
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Chapter 1: Computer Security Fundamentals
Definitions and challenges of computer security
Chapter 2: Introduction to Cryptography
Cryptography objectives
Symmetric/asymmetric cryptography
Cryptographic hash functions
Electronic signature: general principles
Basic concepts in key management
Standards overview (AES, RSA, SHA, etc.)
Chapter 3: Advanced Encryption Methods
One-way functions
RSA method and integer factorization
Discrete logarithm and ElGamal cryptosystem
Knapsack problem
Error-correcting codes and McEliece cryptosystem
Elliptic curves and ECC cryptosystems
Secret sharing (Shamir, etc.)
Image encryption
Copyright protection
New trends in cryptography
Introduction to cryptanalysis
Chapter 4: Authentication and Integrity
Authentication protocol principles
Authentication techniques
Digital signature and certificates
Public key signatures
File security: integrity, access, confidentiality
Examples and algorithms (HMAC, RSA-Sign, etc.)
Chapter 5: Virology and Software Security
Introduction to computer virology
Replication and propagation techniques
Malware typologies (viruses, worms, Trojans, ransomware...)
Protection and prevention methods
Antivirus products: typologies and functionalities
Introduction to malware analysis
Chapter 6: Network Security and Firewalls
Firewall technologies
Firewall placement and role in a network
Security policy and rule configuration
Introduction to IDS/IPS
Practical firewall configuration example
Chapter 7: Network Security Infrastructure and Policies
Network security infrastructure: firewalls, WAF, proxy, NAC, servers, IDPS, DMZ, VPN
Network security policies: Zero Trust, SIEM, IDS/IPS, access security, vulnerability management, audit, compliance, user awareness
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
Laurent Bloch, Christophe Wolfhugel, "Sécurité Informatique: Principes et Méthode", Eyrolles 2007.
Hans Delfs, Helmut Knebl, "Introduction to Cryptography: Principles and Applications", 2nd Edition, Springer 2007.
Stallings, W., Cryptography and Network Security: Principles and Practice, 7th ed. Pearson (2016).
A. Menezes, P. van Oorschot, and S. Vanstone, Handbook of Applied Cryptography, CRC Press (1996).
Ireland & Rosen, A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory, Springer.
Koblitz, A Course in Number Theory and Cryptography, Springer, 1994.
Blake, Seroussi et Smart, Elliptic Curves in Cryptography, Springer.
Takanori Isobe, Santanu Sarkar, Progress in Cryptology, Springer International Publishing, 2022.
W. Stallings. "Computer Security: Principles and Practice". Prentice Hall. 2011.
Didier Müller. Les Codes secrets décryptés. City Editions, 2007.
Renaud Dumont: Cryptographie et Sécurité informatique. Université de Liège 2010.
Touradj Ebrahimi, Franck Leprévost, Bertrand Warusfel, Cryptographie et sécurité des systèmes et réseaux, Hermès - Lavoisier 2006.
M. T. Goodrich, R. Tamassia. "Introduction to Computer Security". Pearson, 2010.
G. Avoine, P. Junod, P. Oechslin. "Sécurité Informatique". Vuibert. 2010.
